payload.message: Run this prompt file: cron/MYSQL_NEW_USERS.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read cron/MYSQL_NEW_USERS.md. Then run the Python helper exactly. If the helper prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If it returns JSON with status OK, use only that compact JSON to return one line per user exactly like: Dripr has a new signup! <email> registered on Month D @ HH:MM. Format creation_datetime with the full English month name, unpadded day, 24-hour hour, and two-digit minute, for example May 10 @ 18:16. If it returns SETUP_REQUIRED or ERROR, return only the explicit failure line from the prompt. Do not mention prompts, files, tools, SQL, credentials, hostnames, internal process, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/DRIPR_INBOX_TRIAGE.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read cron/DRIPR_INBOX_TRIAGE.md. Then use exec to run this exact command with no extra arguments: python3 capabilities/dripr_inbox_triage/dripr_inbox_triage.py process. Return only the helper stdout as final visible text. If the helper prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. Only if the exec command exits non-zero, return exactly Mira dripr inbox triage failed: Gmail unavailable. Do not mention prompts, files, tools, JSON, cron, internal process, or reasoning.

Run this prompt file: cron/CLOUDWATCH_DASHBOARD.md. This is a cron execution job. Your first tool call must read cron/CLOUDWATCH_DASHBOARD.md. Then run python3 capabilities/cloudwatch_dashboard/cloudwatch_dashboard.py review exactly. If the helper prints NO_REPLY, return exactly NO_REPLY. If it returns JSON with status OK, use only that compact JSON to return a short Telegram-ready alert naming the CloudWatch dashboard checks that need attention. If it returns SETUP_REQUIRED or ERROR, return only the explicit failure line from the prompt. Do not mention prompts, files, tools, JSON, AWS credentials, account IDs, ARNs, dashboard internals, internal process, or reasoning.
